hypothesis: If we compute CKNNA, IDCor, and Information Imbalance between last-layer embeddings of diverse scientific foundation models on shared molecule, material, and protein datasets, then we will observe significant cross-model alignment indicative of a common low-dimensional manifold—strongest for equivariant geometry-based models and in-distribution data and weaker on out-of-distribution inputs—because these models learn shared physico-chemical constraints of matter despite differing modalities and architectures.
